text.title: Scrum Ceremonies Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs a symptom (observable signal) with root cause and concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team runs all five events but DoR/DoD discipline and action follow-through are absent.
antipattern.root-cause: no DoR check at planning; no owner on retro actions
antipattern.fix: Wire DoR gate into Planning; reject unowned retro actions.

antipattern.symptom: Standup updates go to a manager dashboard rather than the team channel.
antipattern.root-cause: tool destination is a manager-only board
antipattern.fix: Move standup output to the team channel; manager pulls from there.

antipattern.symptom: Same retro actions appear sprint after sprint without resolution.
antipattern.fix: Make previous-action review the first retro agenda item; close before adding new.

antipattern.symptom: PO names a number first and team votes match it; poker yields no diversity.
antipattern.fix: Mask PO/agent estimates until after first vote.
